summary: Excellent soundboard recording of an 8/10/82 Iowa City show – crystal‑clear mix and a fireworks‑like set highlighted by a smoking Eyes solo, soaring Iko Iko and a blistering Bertha.
review: Fans rave that this is one of the best Dead shows of the ’80s. The soundboard capture is crisp and balanced (aside from a brief PA hiccup in the first set), letting Jerry’s legendary second‑solo on “Eyes” shine, while the second set rolls through classics like “Iko Iko,” “Stella Blue,” “Sugar Magnolia” and a monster “Bertha.” The energy is nonstop and the band feels locked in.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 90 % of reviews are 5‑star, repeatedly praising both performance and sound quality; only isolated minor complaints exist.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with crisp highs and tight low end. A few reviewers note a PA distortion in the first six songs that clears up after the early set break.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds energy despite a brief sound‑issue, then the second set launches into a seamless run of high‑octane jams (China Cat>Rider, Lost Sailor>Saint, Eyes) and closes with a fire‑filled Bertha and an exuberant encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone and improvisational solo on “Eyes” are repeatedly called spectacular; vocals described as mellow yet powerful.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is generally praised for its groove, though one reviewer noted the mix buried his low end a bit. Phil’s presence backstage (opening doors) is fondly remembered.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s vocal delivery on “Iko Iko” and “Baby Blue” gets mixed remarks – some say he drowns out Jerry, others love his raspy intensity.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives strong praise for keyboard work and vocal harmonies ("nicelike" singing). Keith Godchaux is mentioned only briefly in a nostalgic sense.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s snare kick on the Eyes solo and Bill’s steady drive are highlighted; one reviewer applauds the drum‑channel separation in the mix.
